events.title: 📧 PASSWORD RESET ALERT
narrative: Click the link NOW or lose access.

OPEN LINK.message: You fell for urgency manipulation! The domain 'c0mpany.net' was fake — attackers use fake deadlines to bypass rational thinking.

IGNORE.message: Ignoring is better than clicking, but reporting protects the whole team!

events.success_message: Excellent! Verifying the sender domain revealed it was fake. Always check the full email address!

events.title: 📋 CAFETERIA POSTER
narrative: Scan QR code to claim yours.

SCAN QR CODE.message: Reward manipulation! The QR led to a credential-harvesting site. Attackers use prizes to bypass critical thinking.

events.success_message: Smart! Always verify QR codes. 'hr-certs.xyz' is not the company domain — classic impersonation.

events.title: 💾 SUSPICIOUS USB FOUND
narrative: You spot a USB drive on the floor.

PLUG IN & OPEN.message: USB Drop Attack! Attackers plant baited drives. Yours just ran a keylogger. Never plug in unknown devices!

events.success_message: Correct! USB drops are a classic physical attack. The IT team found malware inside — great instinct!

events.title: 💻 URGENT HR MESSAGE
narrative: Failure to submit may affect record.

OPEN LINK.message: Authority phishing! 'hr-portal-forms.net' is not the company domain. Verify ALL HR requests directly in person.

events.success_message: Well done! HR confirmed they sent no such email. The link was a credential-harvesting page.

events.title: 📞 INCOMING CALL
narrative: 'Verify your identity — give us the OTP
sent to your phone. NOW please!'

SHARE OTP.message: Vishing attack! IT NEVER asks for OTPs by phone. Your account is now compromised. -15 points!

events.success_message: Perfect! Legitimate IT staff NEVER ask for OTPs over the phone. You correctly identified vishing!

events.title: 🚨 CEO: URGENT REQUEST

APPROVE TRANSFER.message: CEO Fraud (BEC Attack)! $50,000 lost. The real CEO never emailed this. Always verify financial requests via phone!

events.success_message: BOSS LEVEL CLEARED! You identified a Business Email Compromise attack. The CEO confirmed: they never sent this!
